warnings: Franchisor has only one fiscal period (inception March 6, 2025 to December 31, 2025); no multi-year financial comparison available.
No franchised outlets exist yet; Item 19 data is based solely on one affiliate-owned company location.
Trademarks are pending (not yet federally registered), creating potential brand risk.
Auditor emphasis-of-matter paragraph notes extensive affiliate transactions that may make financials non-indicative of standalone operations.

extracted.brandBackground: DDH (Doctors of Domestic Harmony) is a home organizing and move management franchise launched in March 2025, built on the operating history of affiliate Done and Done NYC LLC, which has operated in the New York metropolitan area since October 2014.
extracted.conceptRationale: DDH provides professional home organizing, estate clean-out, move management, and home sale preparation services delivered at clients' residences — a home/trade services model with no retail storefront required.
extracted.staffingRationale: Item 15 allows an absentee owner with an approved Operating Manager; the affiliate's Item 19 data shows only 2.63 FTE home organizers supporting $1.36M in revenue, indicating a small-crew model.

leadership.name: Kate Pawlowski
leadership.background: Co-CEO of DDH Franchising, LLC and DDH IP, LLC since March 2025; Co-Founder of Done and Done NYC since October 2014.
leadership.whyItMatters: Primary franchisor contact and co-architect of the DDH system with over a decade of operating experience.

leadership.name: Ann Lightfoot
leadership.background: Co-CEO of DDH Franchising, LLC and DDH IP, LLC since March 2025; Co-Founder of Done and Done NYC since October 2014.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees initial franchisee training and co-leads system development alongside Kate Pawlowski.

leadership.name: Josh Cohen
leadership.background: Franchise Coach at SMB Franchise Advisors since February 2026; previously Founder/CEO of The Junkluggers (2004–2024).
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings direct franchise scaling experience from a comparable home-services brand.

leadership.name: Steven J. Berger
leadership.background: Venture capital investor since July 2021; previously Co-Founder, Vice Chairman and CFO of Imagine Learning Inc. (2010–2021).
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides financial and capital-markets advisory perspective to the early-stage franchisor.

item19.notes: All Item 19 data is from a single affiliate-owned outlet (Done and Done NYC LLC) for the 12-month period ending December 31, 2025; no franchised outlet data exists. The affiliate operates in the NYC metro area with ~200,000 qualifying households, which may not be representative of other markets.

cohorts.label: Company-Owned Outlet – Gross Revenue (12 months ending Dec 31, 2025)
cohorts.basis: Actual gross revenue reported by Done and Done NYC LLC for the 12-month period ending December 31, 2025.

cohorts.label: Company-Owned Outlet – Gross Profit Less Disclosed Expenses and Franchisee-Related Expenses
cohorts.basis: Gross profit ($717,887) less disclosed operating expenses ($269,511) and pro-forma franchisee-related adjustments ($169,503) including royalty, brand fund, local advertising, technology fee, sales support, and back office fees.

cohorts.label: Average Gross Revenue by All Clients
cohorts.basis: Average gross revenue per client engagement across all 179 clients in 2025; average $7,577.66 per client, median $5,522, highest $105,706, lowest $400.

cohorts.label: Average Gross Revenue by Repeat Clients
cohorts.basis: Average gross revenue per repeat client engagement; average $8,965.18, median $5,382, highest $31,816, lowest $740.

cohorts.label: Average Gross Revenue by First-Time Clients
cohorts.basis: Average gross revenue per first-time client engagement; average $6,665.49, median $5,913, highest $105,706, lowest $400.

hiddenCosts.name: Sales Support Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 2.5% of Gross Revenue paid weekly to franchisor for sales support center services; debited automatically via ACH.

hiddenCosts.name: Accounting Fee (Mandatory Designated Vendor)
hiddenCosts.description: $500/month paid to franchisor's designated accounting vendor (currently QMK Consulting); mandatory for all franchisees.

hiddenCosts.name: Third-Party Software Licenses (QBOE, Google Suite, Slack, MailChimp)
hiddenCosts.description: Estimated $300/month for required software licenses designated by franchisor.

hiddenCosts.name: Convention/Conference Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Up to $1,500 per attendee; franchisee must pay for two attendees even if absent.

hiddenCosts.name: Computer System Upgrades
hiddenCosts.description: Estimated $0–$2,000/year for required updates and modifications to the Computer System.

hiddenCosts.name: Post-Termination Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Non-refundable fee equal to 3% of total Gross Revenue in the last 24 months, payable upon termination or expiration.

hiddenCosts.name: Pre-Mailer Marketing Materials
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisor sends marketing pre-mailers to customers before each appointment and charges franchisee for the cost.

hiddenCosts.name: Non-Compliance Fees
hiddenCosts.description: Up to $1,000 per occurrence for reporting, operations, or payment non-compliance.

operationalRisks.title: Short Operating History
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor was formed in March 2025 and has zero franchised outlets; the system is entirely unproven at scale.

operationalRisks.title: Minimum Sales Performance Requirements
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee must achieve $100K (Year 3), $150K (Year 4), and $200K+ (Year 5+) annual gross revenue per territory or risk territory reduction or termination.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Personal Guarantee
operationalRisks.description: Married franchisees must have their spouse sign a personal guarantee making both jointly and severally liable for all franchise obligations.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: All disputes must be litigated or arbitrated in Montclair, New Jersey, which may disadvantage out-of-state franchisees.

operationalRisks.title: Unregistered Trademarks
operationalRisks.description: DDH and DOCTORS OF DOMESTIC HARMONY marks are pending (not federally registered), reducing legal protections and creating potential rebranding risk.

operationalRisks.title: Extensive Affiliate Transactions
operationalRisks.description: Auditor's emphasis-of-matter notes extensive transactions with affiliates, meaning franchisor financials may not reflect standalone operations.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Has No Franchise Revenue Yet
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor generated $0 revenue in its first operating period (March–December 2025), relying on member contributions to fund operations.

operationalRisks.title: Item 19 Based on Single Affiliate Location
operationalRisks.description: Financial performance data comes from one affiliate-owned NYC metro location; results may not be representative of franchisee markets.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Designated Accounting Vendor
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must use franchisor's designated accounting vendor (QMK Consulting) at $500/month with no competitive bidding allowed.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Right to Modify Business Model
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may modify manuals, marks, system, and approved goods/services without franchisee consent, potentially requiring additional investment.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p.7 — rent deposit estimated at one month's rent; no per-sqft rate disclosed. Item 11 notes minimum 100 sq ft required.

riskReasons: 4 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $89,523 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $100,991/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: DDH provides professional home organizing, estate clean-out, move management, and home sale preparation services delivered at clients' residences — a home/trade services model with no retail storefront required.

benchmark.label: Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Crew utilization, callback/warranty cost, and customer-acquisition cost. Low fixed overhead, but CAC and scheduling efficiency decide profitability.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–9 months to a steady job pipeline.
considerations: Budget materials/subcontractor COGS ~25–40% and field labor ~20–35%.
CAC (lead cost per booked job) is the dominant hidden line — ask Item 20 operators what they actually spend per job.
Confirm territory size, lead-generation expectations, and any required call-center/marketing fees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 10–21% — the 20–35% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: lightly staffed · ≈ 3.3–6.8 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 2–6% category band.
Estimated at the 25–40% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 10–21% lightly staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: This franchisor is losing money as it grows (a net loss of $60K in the most recent year), though it still has positive net worth. Worth understanding how long its runway lasts.
body: What we found (2025-12-31, audited by Metwally CPA PLLC): Net loss of $60K on $0 of revenue. Positive net worth of $106K. About 99% of liabilities is money owed to related parties — the entity is kept alive largely by insider loans, not profit. Cash of $251K against $144K of obligations due within a year (a current ratio of 1.74).
Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: heavy dependence on related-party loans to stay funded
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
